duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Uncapped TGT deposits via onTokenTransfer allow totalTitnClaimable to exceed TITN_ARB, causing protocol insolvency and bank-run dynamics where early claimers drain the pool
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 12 threats describe the identical root cause: onTokenTransfer increments totalTitnClaimable without any cap check against TITN_ARB or actual TITN balance. ECON-H001 selected as representative for its comprehensive attack economics, victim impact analysis, and clear explanation of the bank-run dynamic. Note: TECH2-H003 appears twice in input with duplicate IDs; the 'Unbounded TGT intake' variant belongs to this group.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Arithmetic underflow in withdrawRemainingTitn when remainingTitnAfter1Year < initialTotalClaimable permanently DoS-es all post-360-day claims, including the special case where balance==0 causes perpetual re-initialization
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 7 threats target the same subtraction `unclaimedTitn = remainingTitnAfter1Year - initialTotalClaimable` in withdrawRemainingTitn(). TECH-H007 describes the specific zero-balance re-initialization edge case, which is a sub-case of the same underflow. ECON-H002 selected for its detailed cascading analysis linking to insolvency and comprehensive victim impact assessment.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Permanent isBridgedTokenHolder flag set by _credit enables cross-chain dust bridging griefing that permanently restricts victim transfers, with no admin clearing mechanism
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 11 threats describe the same root cause: _credit unconditionally sets isBridgedTokenHolder[_to]=true with no reversal mechanism, enabling dust-amount griefing. OPS-H006 focuses on the lack of admin remediation, which is the same code defect viewed from an operational perspective. ECON-H004 selected for its comprehensive attack economics ($0.05-$0.50 per victim), detailed preconditions, and clear permanent impact analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Off-by-one boundary at exactly 360 days: onTokenTransfer accepts deposit (> 360d check) but quoteTitn returns 0 (< 360d check), permanently trapping user TGT with zero TITN output
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 9 threats describe the identical boundary check inconsistency between onTokenTransfer (> 360 days), quoteTitn (< 360 days), and claimTitn (>= 360 days). TECH-H004 selected for its precise line references across all three functions and clearest explanation of the inconsistent comparison operators. Note: TECH2-H004 appears twice in input with duplicate IDs; the 'Off-By-One Boundary' variant belongs to this group while the 'Owner renounce ownership' variant is a distinct unique threat.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Dead lzEndpoint transfer exemption in _validateTransfer serves no bridging purpose (OFT uses _burn) and creates a permanent token loss vector for users transferring directly to the endpoint address
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 8 threats describe the same code defect: _validateTransfer exempts `to == lzEndpoint` but OFT bridging uses _burn (bypassing transfer), making the exemption dead code that only enables permanent token loss. ECON-H006 selected for its thorough analysis of why the exemption is functionless for legitimate bridging and its clear impact assessment. Note: TECH2-H003 appears twice in input; the 'Misleading LZ Endpoint Exemption' variant belongs to this group.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Missing minimum output validation in onTokenTransfer allows zero-TITN deposits during late decay phase due to integer division truncation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same missing check: onTokenTransfer does not validate titnOut > 0 after calling quoteTitn, allowing deposits during the late decay period to yield zero TITN due to integer truncation. Distinct from DUP-004 (boundary mismatch at exactly 360d) as this covers the general decay period. ECON-H005 selected for its more detailed analysis of the rounding mechanics and broader time range coverage.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Division-by-zero in withdrawRemainingTitn when initialTotalClaimable snapshots to 0, permanently bricking the proportional share calculation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 3 threats describe the same code defect: `(claimableTitn * unclaimedTitn) / initialTotalClaimable` has no guard for initialTotalClaimable == 0. Distinct from DUP-002 (underflow in subtraction) as this is a different arithmetic failure mode in the same function. TECH-H014 selected for its analysis of reachability conditions. Note: TECH2-H005 appears twice in input; the 'Division-by-zero' variant belongs to this group.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Integer division truncation in withdrawRemainingTitn proportional share calculation leaves dust TITN permanently locked in the contract
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 3 threats describe the same truncation issue: `(claimableTitn * unclaimedTitn) / initialTotalClaimable` rounds down per-user, causing cumulative remainder to be permanently locked. ECON-H013 selected for clearest explanation of the cumulative truncation effect. Note: TECH2-H005 appears twice in input; the 'Proportional Share Truncation' variant belongs to this group.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: OFT bridge-out via send()/_burn bypasses _validateTransfer restrictions, undermining transfer lock assumptions on Arbitrum
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same control gap: _validateTransfer only applies to transfer/transferFrom, but OFT.send() uses internal _burn which bypasses these overrides entirely. OPS-H014 selected for its more detailed operational context and config evidence from deployment scripts.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: onTokenTransfer trusts the 'from' parameter from TGT ERC677 callback without independent verification, potentially allowing misattribution of claimable TITN if TGT allows arbitrary 'from'
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same trust issue: claimableTitnPerUser[from] is credited based on an unverified parameter from the TGT token callback. OPS2-H010 selected for its more comprehensive analysis of the ERC677 calling convention semantics and attack scenarios.
